channel.title: Pie on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Pie on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: Sugar Pie
item.description:
Submitted by Heather Bell

Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 ½ cup brown sugar
  • 1 ½ tbsp flour

Instructions

Place the above ingredients in a saucepan. Add 1 cup of canned milk. Cook over low heat, stirring until dissolved. Remove from heat. Slowly add 1 beaten egg, stirring constantly. Pour into an 8 inch, unbaked pie shell. Bake at 400 degrees for 10 minutes. Reduce heat to 300 degrees and continue baking for 30 minutes, or until thick.

item.title: Meat Pies
item.description:
Submitted by Del Gosson

Aunt of the Bride

Filling:

  • 3 lbs of beef, or cheaper cut of steak
  • 2 lbs of pork, remove fat
  • 2 double chicken breasts, cooked for about 20 minutes
  • 3 large onions
  • 1 tbsp salt
  • 1 tsp pepper
  • ½ cup flour

Pie Crust:

Ingredients

  • 1 lb of shortening

Instructions

Follow pastry recipe on shortening box. Add 2 extra cups of flour and enough water to hold the pastry together.

item.title: Flapper Pie Special
item.description:
Submitted by Gisele and Ronnie Roy

Cousins of the Bride

  • 18-20 graham wafers
  • ¼ cup butter, melted
  • ¼ cup shortening, melted
  • ¼ tsp cinnamon

Crush and roll wafers fine. Blend in butter, shortening and cinnamon. Line bottom and sides of a 9 inch pie plate. Reserve 1/8 cup for topping.

Filling:

Ingredients

  • 4 eggs
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 1 ½ tbsp cornstarch
  • 2 cups milk
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Instructions

Separate eggs. Beat yolks until lemony in color. Add sugar and cornstarch and blend well.

item.title: Deluxe Pumpkin Cream Cheese Pie
item.description:
Submitted by Charleen and Jim Martin

Cousins of the Bride

Requires 2 uncooked pie shells.

  • 1 large pkg cream cheese
  • 1 egg
  • ½ cup white sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Beat the above ingredients together and spread in bottom of pie shells.

Pumpkin Layer:

Ingredients

  • 2 cups pumpkin, canned
  • 2 beaten eggs
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • 1 cup milk
  • ½ tsp ginger

Instructions

Mix ingredients and spread carefully over cream cheese layer in pie shells.

item.title: Apple Pie
item.description:
Submitted by Yolande and Dave Worthington

Aunt and Uncle of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 4 VERY large Granny Smith apples (6 cups)
  • 2/3 to 1 cup white sugar, depending on tartness of apples
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • 1/8 tsp salt
  • 1 tbsp cornstarch

Instructions

Prepare the “Never Fail Pastry” on page 92. Use 2 portions.

Mix sugar, cinnamon, salt and cornstarch together. Peel and thinly slice the apples. Toll out one portion of the pastry and line the bottom of a 9 inch pie pan. Place alternate layers of apples and sugar mixture in the pie pan. Roll out the second portion of pastry, cut vents and place top crust in position. Seal edge and flute.

item.title: Salmon Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 15-1/2-oz tin canned salmon
  • 1 cup liquid (liquid from salmon plus milk)
  • ½ cup soft bread crumbs
  • 1 tsp grated onions
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 tsp lemon juice
  • 1 tbsp chopped parsley
  • 2 eggs, slightly beaten
  • ½ tsp garlic salt

Instructions

Flake salmon and add salmon liquid and milk. Stir in remaining ingredients and mix well.

Pour into a greased 8-inch pie pan and bake in a 350 degree oven for 45 – 50 minutes or until center is solid when tested with a knife.

item.title: Pizza Pie Dough
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cup Bisquick
  • 1/3 cup very hot water

Instructions

Heat oven to 450 degrees. Grease cookie sheet or 12-inch pizza pan. Mix 1-1/2 cups Bisquick baking mix and 1/3 cup very hot water; beat vigorously 20 strokes. Turn dough onto surface well dusted with baking mix. Knead about 60 times or until smooth and no longer sticky. With hands dusted with baking mix, press dough into 13-inch circle on cookie sheet or press in pizza pan; pinch edge, forming ½ inch rim. Top pizza to taste. Bake 12 to 15 minutes or until crust is brown and cheese is melted and bubbly.

item.title: Cheese Krispies
item.description:
This recipe came from Pat Johnston, who is Yolande’s Friend

Ingredients

  • 1 cup Butter or Margarine
  • 1 Pkg (1/2 lb.) Imperial Cheese (Red)
  • Dash Cayenne Pepper
  • Pinch of salt
  • ½ tsp Worcestershire Sauce
  • 1-1/2 cups Flour
  • 4 cups Rice Krispies

Instructions

Combine all ingredients by hand or with a pastry blender. Form into small balls. Place on cookie sheet and flatten with fork. Bake 15 – 20 minutes at 350 degrees.

item.title: Hurry Up Chicken Pot Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2cups chopped cooked chicken breasts
  • 2hard-boiled eggs, sliced
  • 1⁄2cup thinly slicedcarrot
  • 1⁄2cupfrozen green pea
  • 1(10 3/4 ounce) can cream of chicken soup
  • 1cupchicken broth
  • salt and pepper (optional)
  • 11⁄2cups instantbiscuit mix
  • 1cupmilk
  • 1⁄2cup meltedbutter

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.
  2. In a greased 2-quart casserole, layer the chicken, eggs, carrots, and peas.
  3. Mix the soup, chicken broth, and season with salt and pepper, if desired; pour over the layers.
  4. Stir together the biscuit mix and milk, and pour this over the casserole.
  5. Drizzle butter over the topping.
  6. Bake until the topping is golden brown, 30 to 40 minutes.

Credit for this recipe goes to Paula Deen:

item.title: Apple Cider Rice Krispie Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/2cupunsalted butter, plus more for greasing
  • 2 cupsapple cider or unfiltered apple juice
  • 1 tablespoongranulated sugar
  • 1 teaspoonground cinnamon
  • 210-ounce bags mini marshmallows, divided (about 11 cups total)
  • 1/4teaspoonkosher salt
  • 112-ounce box rice cereal, such as Rice Krispies (about 10 cups)
  • Nonstick cooking spray

Instructions

  1. In a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven set over medium heat, melt the butter. Continue cooking, stirring often to prevent the milk solids from burning, until the butter foams and darkens into a light amber color and smells nutty, 4 to 6 minutes. Immediately add the apple cider, increase the heat to high, and bring to a boil. Let the cider boil, stirring occasionally, until it reduces into a thick, bubbling caramel-like consistency that coats the back of a spoon, 18 to 20 minutes.
  2. As the cider is reducing, grease a 9x13-inch glass or metal baking pan. In a small bowl, combine the sugar and cinnamon.
  3. Once the cider has reduced, add one bag of marshmallows and salt and continue cooking over high heat, stirring constantly, until the marshmallows melt and darken into a light caramel color, 3 to 4 minutes.
  4. Remove the pot from the heat and add the remaining bag of marshmallows. Stir just until the marshmallows are about half melted, then add the rice cereal. Gently but quickly mix everything together until the rice cereal is evenly coated in the marshmallow mixture and immediately transfer into the prepared baking pan. Gently press the mixture into an even layer using a spatula sprayed with nonstick cooking spray. Sprinkle the cinnamon sugar mixture over the top and let set for at least 1 hour before cutting.

Credit for this recipe goes to Food52.com:

item.title: Keto Meat Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

Pie Contents

  • ½ yellow onion, finely chopped
  • 1 garlic clove, finely chopped
  • 2 tbsp butter or olive oil
  • 20 oz. ground beef or ground lamb
  • salt and pepper
  • 1 tbsp dried oregano or dried basil
  • 4 tbsp tomato paste or ajvar relish
  • ½ cup water

Pie crust

  • ¾ cupalmond flour
  • 4 tbsp sesame seeds
  • 4 tbspcoconut flour
  • 1 tbsp ground psyllium husk powder
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 1 pinch salt
  • 3 tbsp olive oil or coconut oil
  • 1 egg
  • 4 tbsp water

Topping

item.title: Seapie (Yan's)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 pack of beef
  • 1 pack of pork
  • 1/2 turkey breast or 1 chicken breast
  • 1 can of beef consommé
  • 1 can of water
  • 1/2 medium onion
  • 2 tbsp lard
  • Bannock (double batch)  Recipe here:Bannock (Yan’s)

Instructions

  1. Lard up the sides of the cast iron dutch oven.
  2. Cut meat into bite-sized cubes and toss that shit into the pot.
  3. Chop up the stupid onions into small enough pieces so you won’t know it’s there.  Mix that devil’s veggie witht he meat chunks.
  4. Salt and pepper that bitch, but keep in mind the consommé is salty, so don’t overdo it.
  5. Throw in lard tbsps over top the meaty mix.
  6. Add in consommé and water.  Should be covering meaty mix but just barely.
  7. Mix up the bannock dough and form a flat circle the size of the pot.  It won’t shrink or grow, so make it pretty close.
  8. Slap that doughy deliciousness over to the meaty mix, then cut some air vent slits into it.  Just a few - no need to go full Dahmer on it.
  9. Splash a bit of water over the bannock so that it doesn’t burn or get drier than a bucket of sand.
  10. Put it in the oven at 350 degrees for about 4 hours or so.
